title: Repository Layout Model
description: Framework §4.3. A glob-based declaration of which files are legal where. Allowlist semantics: every file must match an allow rule (no-orphans) or fail. Two normative guards: every rule cites the principle it enforces; explicit prohibitions are reserved for actively-dangerous cases.

archetype.description: The archetype this layout belongs to (optional at framework level).

allowlist.description: Allowlist semantics. When true (the default and recommended), a file matching no allow rule fails (no-orphans).

rule.description: Exactly one rule-kind key (must_exist | may_exist_here | must_co_exist | must_not_exist | no_orphans) must be present.

enforces.description: Guard 1 (normative): the principle(s)/top-decision(s) this rule protects. Required on every rule, and ESPECIALLY on must_not_exist. A prohibition with no principle is a superstition.

must_exist.description: A glob that must be matched by a required file. Proactive: fires on absence.

cardinality.description: Required when must_exist is set. 'exactly 1' = a global singleton (zero AND two both fail); '1 per scope' uses for_each.

for_each.description: A parent glob; a '1 per scope' must_exist is quantified over each match. Use {dir} in must_exist to refer to the scope match.

may_exist_here.description: The legal placement(s) for a file type. A matching file appearing elsewhere fails.

must_co_exist.description: Completeness: when a file/dir matches 'when', the 'require' globs must also be present in the same scope.

must_not_exist.description: A forbidden file/pattern. Reactive: fires on presence. Guard 2: reserve for actively-dangerous cases that deserve a named error; the allowlist handles ordinary 'not permitted'.

scope.description: Optional scope to which a must_not_exist or may_exist_here applies (e.g. '/' for the repo root).

no_orphans.description: A scope glob (e.g. 'src/**') within which every file must match at least one allow rule. Anchors allowlist semantics.

then.description: Guard 1 applies with extra force to prohibitions.
